_provenance.method: Stewart (1977) yield response vs Michigan climate variability
references: Stewart JI et al. (1977) Utah Water Research Lab, PRWG 151-1
Doorenbos J, Kassam AH (1979) FAO Irrig Drain Paper 33
Allen RG et al. (1998) FAO-56 Chapter 10, Table 24
_provenance.data_source: FAO-56 Table 24 (Ky), synthetic Michigan weather (reproducible RNG)
_provenance._tolerance_justification: Stewart model is empirical (Ky from field trials); yield ratios validated to 0.01 precision against analytical expectations

multi_year._note: 20 years of rainfed corn on loam with variable drought. Low yield ratios are expected for rainfed conditions with no irrigation — Michigan corn achieves ~0.85-0.95 Ya/Ymax only with irrigation or in exceptionally wet years. Rainfed stress days are high because cumulative ET exceeds rainfall over the full 150-day season.

thresholds._tolerance_justification: Physical bounds from Stewart equation: Ya/Ymax in [0,1], ETa/ETc in [0,1]
